standard_description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present, and future (e.g., Yesterday I walked home; Today I walk home; Tomorrow I will walk home).
subject: Language

items.question_content:
**Which word in this sentence is a verb?**

>Jake lifted the chrome panel.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Verbs are **actions**.  Which word in this sentence tells you **what action is being done**?
The action being done in this sentence is **lifting**. The verb is **"lifted"**.

khan_exercises.title: Introduction to verb agreement
khan_exercises.description: Choose the appropriate form of the verb by asking yourself: "How many things is the sentence about?"

items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank with the verb that fits best.**

>We  \_\_\_\_\_\_ at dawn.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Verbs** must *agree* with their **subjects**. So plural subjects (like *we*, *they*, or *elephants*) need plural verbs, while singular subjects (like *I*, *you*, or *elephant*) need singular verbs.

For example:

* The elephant **eats**.
* The elephants **eat**.
In this sentence, **"we"** is a plural subject. It needs a plural verb, so the correct form is **"leave"**.

items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank with the verb that fits best.**

>Muneera  \_\_\_\_\_\_ a painting on the wall.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Verbs** must *agree* with their **subjects**. So plural subjects (like *we*, *they*, or *elephants*) need plural verbs, while singular subjects (like *I*, *you*, or *elephant*) need singular verbs.

For example:

* The elephant **eats**.
* The elephants **eat**.
In this sentence, **"Muneera"** is a singular subject. A singular subject needs a singular verb, so the correct form is **"adjusts"**.

khan_exercises.title: Introduction to verb tense
khan_exercises.description: Choose the appropriate verb tense by asking yourself: "Does this sentence take place in the past, the present, or the future?"

items.question_content:
**Choose the verb tense that makes the most sense.**

>Yesterday, Ralph  \_\_\_\_\_\_ a donut.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Verb tense** tells us *when* something happens: the **present**, the **past**, or the **future.** 

The tense we choose should match the intended meaning of the sentence.

Remember: **future** tense verbs use the helping verb *"will"*, and **past** tense verbs usually (though not always) end in *-ed*.
This sentence tells us that the action happened "yesterday", so we should use the past tense verb **"ate"**.

items.question_content:
**Choose the verb tense that makes the most sense.**

>Moneta  \_\_\_\_\_\_ to Belgium next week.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Verb tense** tells us *when* something happens: the **present**, the **past**, or the **future.** 

The tense we choose should match the intended meaning of the sentence.

Remember: **future** tense verbs use the helping verb *"will"*, and **past** tense verbs usually (though not always) end in *-ed*.
This sentence tells us that the action will happen "next week", so we should use the future tense verb **"will travel"**.

items.question_content:
**Choose the verb tense that makes the most sense.**

>The dead leaves \_\_\_\_\_\_ while we were gone.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Verb tense** tells us *when* something happens: the **present**, the **past**, or the **future.** 

The tense we choose should match the intended meaning of the sentence.

Remember: **future** tense verbs use the helping verb *"will"*, and **past** tense verbs usually (though not always) end in *-ed*.
This sentence suggests that the action *already* happened ("while we were gone"), so we should use the past tense verb **"rotted"**.

Remember: **future** tense verbs use the helping verb *"will"*, and **past** tense verbs usually (though not always) end in *-ed*.
This sentence suggests that the action *already* happened ("this morning"), so we should use the past tense verb **"knocked"**.

Remember: **future** tense verbs use the helping verb *"will"*, and **past** tense verbs usually (though not always) end in *-ed*.
This sentence suggests that the action *already* happened by using past tense earlier in the sentence ("dropped"). So, we should use the past tense verb **"whacked"**.

[[☃ radio 1]]
